session.user: Run fast CI checks (ruff, ty, pytest unit tests) and report all results. Do NOT fix anything - just report what passes and what fails. Command: make ci-fast
session.assistant: I'll run the fast CI checks and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run fast CI checks (ruff, ty, pytest)

session.tool_result: Exit code 2 make: *** No rule to make target 'ci-fast'. Stop.
session.assistant: Let me check what make targets are available:
session.tool_result:
Usage: make [options] [target] ... Options: -b, -m Ignored for compatibility. -B, --always-make Unconditionally make all targets. -C DIRECTORY, --directory=DIRECTORY Change to DIRECTORY before doing anything. -d Print lots of debugging information. --debug[=FLAGS] Print various types of debugging information. -e, --environment-overrides Environment variables override makefiles. -E STRING, --eval=STRING Evaluate STRING as a makefile statement. -f FILE, --file=FILE, --makefile=FILE Read FILE as a makefile. -h, --help Print this message and exit. -i, --ignore-errors Ignore errors from recipes. -I DIRECTORY, --include-dir=DIRECTORY Search DIRECTORY for included makefiles. -j [N], --jobs[=N] Allow N jobs at once; infinite jobs with no arg. -k, --keep-going Keep going when some targets can't be made. -l [N], --load-average[=N], --max-load[=N] Don't start multiple jobs unless load is below N. -L, --check-symlink-times Use the latest mtime between symlinks and target. -n, --just-print, --dry-run, --recon Don't actually run any recipe; just print them. -o FILE, --old-file=FILE, --assume-old=FILE Consider FILE to be very old and don't remake it. -O[TYPE], --output-sync[=TYPE] Synchronize output of parallel jobs by TYPE. -p, --print-data-base Print make's internal database. -q, --question Run no recipe; exit status says if up to date.
... [17 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Let me check for the Makefile:
session.tool_result:
1→.PHONY: format-check lint prettier prettier-check ty upgrade-ty test py-fast-ci fast-ci all-ci md-check docs-check docs-validate docs-sync-check docs-fix clean publish fix reinstall-erk-tools docs docs-serve docs-deploy exec-reference-check mcp mcp-dev test-erk-mcp docs-v2-build docs-v2-serve pre-push-check install-hooks 2→ 3→prettier: 4→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 5→ 6→prettier-check: 7→ prettier --check '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 8→ 9→format-check: 10→ uv run ruff format --check 11→ 12→lint: 13→ uv run ruff check 14→ 15→fix: 16→ uv run ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes 17→ uv run ruff format 18→ prettier --write '**/*.md' --ignore-path .gitignore 19→ 20→ty: 21→ uv run ty check 22→ 23→upgrade-ty: 24→ uv remove ty --group dev && uv add --dev ty 25→ 26→# === Package-specific test targets === 27→ 28→test-erk-dev: 29→ cd packages/erk-dev && uv run pytest -n auto 30→
... [181 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: I see the correct target is `fast-ci`. Let me run that:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run fast CI checks (ruff, ty, pytest unit tests)

session.assistant:
Perfect! All fast CI checks passed. Here's a summary of the results:
## Fast CI Results: PASSED
All checks completed successfully:
| Check | Result | Details | |-------|--------|---------| | **Lint** | PASSED | All checks passed | | **Format Check** | PASSED | 1727 files already formatted | | **Prettier Check** | PASSED | All matched files use Prettier code style | | **Markdown Check** | PASSED | (no violations) | | **Exec Reference Check** | PASSED | `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` is up to date | | **Type Check (ty)** | PASSED | All checks passed | | **Unit Tests (erk)** | PASSED | 6011 tests in 14.38s | | **Unit Tests (erk-dev)** | PASSED | 136 tests in 5.75s | | **Unit Tests (erk-statusline)** | PASSED | 115 tests in 0.80s | | **Unit Tests (erk-mcp)** | PASSED | All tests passed |
**Total test count: 6,373 tests passed**
**Total time: ~21 seconds**
No issues or failures detected across all fast CI checks.
